title: festuca glauca var. exilior
intro: Festuca glauca var. exilior, also known as blue fescue or mountain blue grass, is a perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health issues such as respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Festuca glauca var. exilior include reducing inflammation, alleviating symptoms of coughs and colds, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be applied topically in the form of salves or creams.
